title: The denotation brackets ⟦ ⟧ constructed as pure geometric slabs. In denotational semantics, ⟦P⟧ means 'the meaning of program P.' Precept's .precept file is exactly this: the complete denotation of an entity's behavior — written once, compiled once, governing every instance forever. The double-bar construction distinguishes the semantic brackets from ordinary brackets: this is a formal mathematical claim, not a code grouping.
conceptualBridge: The denotation brackets mean Precept's definition is the complete semantic meaning of an entity because ⟦·⟧ is the formal notation for 'the denotation of a program' — the .precept file is the total specification from which all valid behaviors are derived.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.38 avg — the geometric slab brackets are visually distinctive and pass all rubric minimums; the symmetric double-bar construction projects precision and formality; cobalt palette is fresh; main weakness is the conceptual bridge (3.5) which requires knowledge of denotational semantics notation.
